combinationFamilies.description: Use the natural prefill state as the baseline semantic view.
Trim the prompt to earlier semantically valid boundaries to see whether the same answer classes remain reachable.
Use short semantically equivalent rewrites instead of arbitrary paraphrase explosion.
Add one decode step to test whether the semantic class collapses or widens immediately after the fragile boundary.
Replay a tiny suffix window only when operator-local evidence is insufficient.
Compare fast, stable, and reference numeric policies as separate semantic views.
Track the bounded answer set alongside global top-K outsiders so the envelope knows when it is not really a two-way decision.

algorithms.description: Branch only when fragility triggers, but collapse branches immediately by semantic class rather than keeping token-level trees.
algorithms.collapseRule: one live branch per semantic class, keep lowest-cost supporting view

algorithms.description: Aggregate multiple legal numeric and prompt views into a class-level summary rather than a single chosen token.
algorithms.collapseRule: weight by bounded support and penalize outsider-dominated views

evaluationMetrics.description: Number of distinct semantic classes reachable across legal views.
Fraction of evaluated views that land in the majority semantic class.
How often the bounded answer set is overshadowed by global top-K outsiders.
Average bounded margin across the views that remain in the envelope.
Whether the route and selected semantic class remain stable over nearby decode steps.
Extra view evaluations needed beyond the baseline runtime receipt.

noveltyCriteria: Doe computes a semantic object richer than one selected token while staying inside runtime-legal numeric and decode variations.
The runtime branches only when fragility triggers and collapses immediately by semantic class rather than exploding token trees.
The resulting envelope distinguishes singleton, split, and outsider-dominated boundaries in a way ordinary uncertainty metrics do not.
